answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Honestly just friends I can grab dinner or coffee with. I moved to Toronto eight months ago and I still don't really have a crew here.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Having people to text when something funny happens, or to make weekend plans with. Back home I had that automatically.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Small, like 4-6 people. I get kind of lost in big groups and end up just talking to whoever I already know.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Pretty easy going. I'm open to meeting up regularly once I feel like people are cool.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: When people are just normal and not trying too hard. Also having something to do helps, like an activity, so there's less pressure to just make conversation.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I'm usually the one making jokes or keeping the energy up. I don't like awkward silences so I try to fill them.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Trying restaurants, going to markets, board games, hiking when the weather's good.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Weekends are best. During the week I'm usually exhausted by the time I'm off work.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Something deeper than surface level. I'm tired of conversations that never go anywhere real.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: People who actually want to know me, not just hang out. I have acquaintances but no one I'd call if I was having a hard time.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: A small group that stays consistent. Like the same people each time so we actually build something.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Slow and steady. I don't open up fast but once I trust people I'm very committed.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: When someone asks me a real question and actually listens to the answer. That goes a long way.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I'm a listener mostly. I pay attention to everyone and I remember stuff people tell me.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Walks, cooking together, book stuff, low-key things where you can actually talk.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Evenings during the week usually work. I keep my weekends a bit flexible but I try not to over-schedule.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Activity friends, basically. Someone to do stuff with.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: A tennis partner and honestly just people to be active with in general.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Doesn't matter much as long as people are outdoorsy or into fitness.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Fast is fine with me.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: Just doing something. Standing around talking is way more awkward for me than being active.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: Organizer type. I usually end up planning stuff.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Running, cycling, tennis, volleyball, that kind of thing.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Early mornings or weekend afternoons.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: I'm not totally sure. Maybe just getting out of the house more? I've been kind of isolated since my breakup.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: A lot. My whole social life was kind of built around my ex and his friends so I'm starting from scratch a bit.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Something low pressure. Like I don't want it to feel like I have to perform or be "on."
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Slow I think. I'm a bit fragile right now, not in a dramatic way, just adjusting.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: Warmth. When people are friendly without being intense.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I'm usually pretty engaged, ask questions, contribute. But it takes me a minute to get there.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Brunch, movies, walks, wine nights at someone's place.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Weekends mostly. I work from home so technically I'm flexible but I like having the structure of keeping weekends for social stuff.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Consistent community. Like a group that meets regularly and I actually know them after a few months.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Routine around people. My work is remote and I can go whole weeks without real social interaction.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Something that has a bit of structure to it so it actually happens and doesn't just fade out.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Steady and regular. I like knowing when the next thing is.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: A clear format helps. When I know what we're doing it's easier to relax.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: tbh I just moved here for a job and know literally nobody so anything lol

answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: friendly people around my age (late 20s) who want to explore the city
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: I'm happy to move fast I have a lot of free time right now

answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: bars, concerts, trying new food, honestly whatever

answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Probably once or twice a month max. I wish I could say more but I'd rather be honest than overcommit.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Honestly just low key hangouts. Nothing too intense.

answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Laid back people who don't take everything too seriously.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Slow is fine. I'm not in a rush.

answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Comedy shows, sports bars, casual hangouts.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: I struggle with social anxiety so I'm looking for something that won't overwhelm me. Small group, relaxed vibe, meaningful over loud.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: A space where I feel like I belong without having to mask. Most social situations feel exhausting for me.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Very small, like 3-5 people absolute max. I shut down in bigger groups.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Slow, very slow. I need to feel safe before I open up.

answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I'm actually pretty warm and funny when I'm comfortable, but it takes time. I'm not a natural first impression person.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: I want a friend group where we actually do things together, not just chat online.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: I have a lot of friends but they're all scattered across different cities. I want local people I can actually see.

answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Warm, established vibe quickly. I'm good at connecting, I just need to meet people.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: I can move fast. I'm an extrovert and I trust my gut on people.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: Honestly I'm pretty comfortable anywhere. I guess just genuine people.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: I'm looking for intellectual companionship I guess. People I can have real conversations with about ideas.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Depth. I have plenty of people to grab drinks with but no one to actually think out loud with.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Curious people, mix of backgrounds. I like when a group has different perspectives.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Gradual is fine. I don't expect intimacy overnight.

answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Fast, very fast. I get bored with slow burn stuff.
